problem: What triggers cancer cells to leave primary tumor and colonize new tissue?
mechanism: Optogenetic control of specific neuron populations
hypothesis: Optogenetic control of specific neuron populations may resolve What triggers cancer cells to leave primary tumor and colonize new tissue? by activating at the molecular level.

experiment.protocol: In vitro cell culture assay with 7 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: The impediment to action advances action. What stands in the way becomes the way. — Marcus Aurelius
